title: lucuma paradoxa
intro: Lucuma paradoxa, also known as the golden cup or lucuma, is a species of flowering plant in the family Sapindaceae. It is native to the Andes mountains in South America, where its edible fruit has been used for centuries in traditional medicine. The medicinal properties of Lucuma paradoxa include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been shown to help soothe digestive issues and protect against cell damage. Herbal preparations of Lucuma paradoxa can be made into teas, tinctures, and powders, which are used to promote overall health and well-being by providing a rich source of vitamins, minerals, and dietary fiber.
